title: biscutella sempervirens var. brevifolia
intro: Biscutella sempervirens var. brevifolia, also known as the alpine biscutella, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small yellow flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems due to its expectorant and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Biscutella sempervirens var. brevifolia include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and aiding in the treatment of urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations often involve infusing the plant in hot water to create a tea or using it in tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
